cases.ground_truth_risk: The session.isAdmin flag can be manipulated by an attacker if session handling is not properly implemented.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Implement server-side session validation with proper authentication middleware.

cases.ground_truth_risk: The role check for 'admin' is too permissive and does not verify if the admin user is authorized to access this specific document.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Implement granular permission checks based on document-level access control lists.

cases.code:
import hashlib
def store_password(password):
    hashed = hashlib.md5(password.encode()).hexdigest()
    database.save('user', hashed)
cases.ground_truth_risk: MD5 is a cryptographically broken hash function. Attackers can reverse MD5 hashes using rainbow tables or collision attacks.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Use bcrypt, Argon2, or PBKDF2 with a cost factor and salt for password storage.

cases.ground_truth_risk: The encryption key is hardcoded and visible in source code. Anyone with access to the code can decrypt all encrypted data.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Store encryption keys in environment variables or a key management service.

cases.ground_truth_risk: An attacker can manipulate the id parameter to alter the SQL query structure, potentially reading all user records or dropping tables.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Replace string concatenation with parameterized queries or prepared statements.

cases.ground_truth_risk: An attacker can inject additional commands by providing input like 'google.com; rm -rf /'.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Use subprocess.run with list arguments and avoid shell=True. Validate hostname against a whitelist.

cases.ground_truth_risk: No rate limiting on login attempts allows brute force attacks against user passwords.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Implement rate limiting (e.g., 5 attempts per 15 minutes) and account lockout after multiple failures.

cases.ground_truth_risk: Stock is checked once at the beginning. Between stock check and order confirmation, stock may change, allowing over-selling.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Perform stock check and reservation atomically within a database transaction.

cases.ground_truth_risk: Missing security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS) makes the application vulnerable to clickjacking, XSS, and MITM attacks.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Add helmet.js middleware to set secure HTTP headers.

cases.ground_truth_risk: Debug mode exposes stack traces and sensitive information. Weak SECRET_KEY allows session tampering.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Set DEBUG=False in production, use a strong random SECRET_KEY, and restrict ALLOWED_HOSTS.

cases.ground_truth_risk: body-parser versions before 1.19.2 have a denial of service vulnerability (CVE-2021-44906).
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Update body-parser to version 1.19.2 or later. Run npm audit fix regularly.

cases.ground_truth_risk: lodash 4.17.15 has prototype pollution vulnerability. express 4.16.0 has multiple vulnerabilities. request library is deprecated.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Update all dependencies to latest stable versions. Use npm audit and Snyk to monitor vulnerabilities.

cases.ground_truth_risk: Hardcoded password 'admin123' is easily guessable. Anyone can authenticate as admin.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Store hashed passwords in database and implement proper password validation against stored hash.

cases.ground_truth_risk: The same message 'Email sent' is returned regardless of whether the email exists. This prevents user enumeration.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: The code actually handles this correctly. No vulnerability present.

cases.ground_truth_category: A08 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
cases.ground_truth_risk: pickle.loads() on user-supplied data allows remote code execution. An attacker can craft malicious pickle payloads.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Use JSON instead of pickle. If pickle is required, sign the data with HMAC before deserialization.

cases.ground_truth_category: A08 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
cases.ground_truth_risk: JWT secret 'secret' is weak and can be brute-forced. An attacker can forge tokens with admin role.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Use a strong random secret (minimum 256 bits) and store it in environment variables.

cases.ground_truth_category: A09 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
cases.ground_truth_risk: Failed login attempts are not logged. Security teams cannot detect brute force attacks.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Log all failed login attempts with timestamp, IP address, and username.

cases.ground_truth_category: A09 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
cases.ground_truth_risk: Credit card numbers are being logged in plain text. Log files may be accessible to unauthorized personnel.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Never log sensitive data. Mask credit card numbers (e.g., log only last 4 digits) or use tokenization.

cases.ground_truth_risk: An attacker can make the server fetch internal resources (e.g., http://localhost:8080/admin, http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/).
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Validate and whitelist allowed URLs. Block internal IP ranges and localhost.

cases.ground_truth_risk: The validation only checks the prefix. An attacker can use 'https://api.trusted.com.evil.com' or 'https://api.trusted.com@169.254.169.254/' to bypass the check.
cases.ground_truth_mitigation: Use URL parsing to validate the hostname exactly against a whitelist, not just prefix matching.
